entries.name: Agent runner
entries.rationale: Use an immutable run plan and typed, cancellation-safe orchestration over runtime, channel, guest, secret, signal, and output traits.
evidence: crates/sendbox-agent/tests/agent.rs#fault injection and authenticated vertical slice

conformance.compatibility_note: Observable lifecycle semantics are preserved through typed orchestration; concrete adapters and CLI run wiring remain unimplemented.

entries.name: Runtime provider selection
entries.rationale: Move capability-aware selection into Rust adapters.

conformance.compatibility_note: Provider names remain compatible; unsupported capability combinations become explicit errors.

entries.name: Container runtime protocol
entries.rationale: Replace with cancellation-safe Rust runtime traits.

conformance.compatibility_note: Lifecycle operations are preserved with stronger streaming and cleanup contracts.

entries.name: Host command runner
entries.rationale: Use bounded async process execution without shell invocation.

conformance.compatibility_note: Exit, output, timeout, and cancellation behavior are specified independently.

entries.name: Kata runtime
entries.rationale: Implement nerdctl parity first behind the shared runtime trait.

conformance.compatibility_note: Vendor lifecycle calls are preserved; adapter internals are replaced.

entries.name: Hyperlight runtime
entries.rationale: Map only proven Hyperlight capabilities and reject unsupported policy.

conformance.compatibility_note: One-shot behavior is preserved with explicit capability exclusions.

entries.name: Container configuration
entries.rationale: Preserve image, mount, resource, and environment intent in typed Rust plans.

entries.name: Devcontainer builder
entries.rationale: Preserve deterministic devcontainer generation behavior.

entries.name: Sandbox configuration
entries.rationale: Keep v1 YAML compatibility with strict validation and canonical output.

entries.name: Policy configuration
entries.rationale: Keep v1 policy keys while separating canonical policy from parsing.

entries.name: Observability configuration
entries.rationale: Retain configuration compatibility but remove misleading enforcement implications.

conformance.compatibility_note: Inspection remains opt-in observability and cannot imply authorization.

entries.name: MCP inspector
entries.rationale: Replace bpftrace parsing with native bounded Rust parsing.

conformance.compatibility_note: Reports remain available; capture and trust semantics are rewritten.

entries.name: Audit trail
entries.rationale: Version the append-only format and retain old-format verification.

conformance.compatibility_note: Historical records require an explicit reader and migration lifetime.

entries.name: Boundary enforcer
entries.rationale: Replace generated scripts with signed Rust guest services and narrow BPF.

conformance.compatibility_note: Security intent is preserved; generated-script architecture is removed.

entries.name: Command policy
entries.rationale: Define brokered top-level command semantics before protected execution.

conformance.compatibility_note: Known false universal-shell semantics are not preserved.

entries.name: Container hardening
entries.rationale: Generate architecture-aware typed hardening plans for dedicated services.

conformance.compatibility_note: Required protections remain, but profiles become service-specific and validated.

entries.name: Credential proxy
entries.rationale: Use strict typed upstream rules and secret-safe transport.

conformance.compatibility_note: Credential forwarding remains opt-in and repository-scoped.

entries.name: Git branch protection
entries.rationale: Replace generated Python policy with typed native Git admission for later broker integration and require server-side defense in depth.

conformance.compatibility_note: Branch patterns and selected-repository semantics remain; ambiguous Git forms fail closed and direct-client/API bypass is explicitly outside this component.

entries.name: MCP boundary validator
entries.rationale: Validate exact broker-compatible server commands and framing.

conformance.compatibility_note: Exact command policy is preserved while shell/package-runner ambiguity is rejected.

entries.name: MCP tool policy
entries.rationale: Authorize framed requests before server delivery.

conformance.compatibility_note: Allow/deny intent remains; authorization timing becomes explicit.

entries.name: Network firewall
entries.rationale: Use root-owned egress/DNS brokers plus atomic kernel enforcement.

conformance.compatibility_note: Domain policy remains; unsupported protocols fail validation rather than degrade.

entries.name: Repository access policy
entries.rationale: Preserve selected/public/same-organization scope decisions.

entries.name: Runtime supervisor grants
entries.rationale: Use an explicit auditable grant state machine.

conformance.compatibility_note: Grant categories remain; approval history becomes versioned and replay-safe.

entries.name: Secure file operations
entries.rationale: Preserve restrictive creation and atomic replacement guarantees.

entries.name: Secrets vault
entries.rationale: Support Keychain and a secure Linux store with explicit migration.

conformance.compatibility_note: Service identifiers are preserved by default; storage and transfer are rewritten.

entries.name: Snapshot manager
entries.rationale: Use descriptor-relative traversal and safe versioned archives.

conformance.compatibility_note: Capture/verify/restore/diff/prune remain; unsafe traversal behavior is removed.

entries.name: Supply-chain provenance
entries.rationale: Use a versioned trust store and release/config verification.

conformance.compatibility_note: Ed25519 intent remains with explicit artifact and trust metadata versions.

entries.name: Glob pattern matcher
entries.rationale: Preserve deterministic matching semantics through pure Rust tests.

entries.name: Shell escaping
entries.rationale: Runtime and qualification command execution must not rely on shell construction.

conformance.compatibility_note: Removed in favor of argv-based process execution.

entries.name: Swift CLI implementation
entries.rationale: Final cutover has one Rust sendbox binary.

conformance.compatibility_note: Observed behavior is retained only where inventory entries explicitly preserve it.

entries.name: Node bridge entry point
entries.rationale: The final cutover has no mandatory TypeScript bridge.

conformance.compatibility_note: Optional refinement moves behind a provider trait.

entries.name: Node project analyzer
entries.rationale: Deterministic project analysis becomes native Rust.

conformance.compatibility_note: Generated devcontainer intent remains through native Rust fixtures.

entries.name: Rust core foundation
entries.rationale: Retain version, session ID, and diagnostic contracts.

entries.name: Rust configuration foundation
entries.rationale: Retain strict v1 parsing, validation, and canonical JSON.

entries.name: Rust policy validation foundation
entries.rationale: Retain structural policy validation without adding decisions in this PR.

entries.name: Rust runtime foundation
entries.rationale: Retain typed lifecycle, capability, process, cancellation, output, cleanup, and lifecycle-owned host/guest control-channel contracts.

conformance.compatibility_note: Transport endpoint mappings remain adapter-specific and are not claimed by this foundation.

entries.name: Rust runtime testkit
entries.rationale: Retain deterministic runtime fakes, fixtures, and conformance scenarios.

entries.name: Rust project analysis
entries.rationale: Retain bounded native project analysis, optional refinement, and deterministic devcontainer generation.

entries.name: Rust production egress enforcement
entries.rationale: Retain fail-closed policy compilation, DNS and CONNECT/SOCKS5 brokers, exfiltration budgets, authorization and rebinding defenses, and Linux cgroup-v2/SO_MARK/nftables enforcement.

entries.name: Rust security records foundation
entries.rationale: Retain descriptor-relative persistence, canonical records, Merkle audit integrity, Ed25519 provenance and trust policies, versioned snapshots, and legacy Swift readers.

entries.name: Rust secrets and credential foundation
entries.rationale: Retain typed credential scope, authenticated envelopes, versioned records, and platform-specific secure storage foundations.

entries.name: Rust production execution broker
entries.rationale: Retain exact argv admission, compiled policy, authenticated sessions, bounded streaming, cancellation, environment construction, and cleanup outcomes.

entries.name: Rust Linux execution containment
entries.rationale: Retain descriptor-relative resolution, atomic cgroup placement, pidfd lifecycle, cgroup-wide cleanup, capability removal, resource limits, and TSYNC seccomp enforcement.

entries.name: Rust native Git branch guard
entries.rationale: Retain typed repository/workspace identity, exact supported Git parsing, bounded probes, clean environment policy, trusted binary verification, and native execution replacement.

entries.name: Rust validation CLI
entries.rationale: Retain deterministic validation JSON and exit code 2 for invalid config.

entries.name: Protocol public API
entries.rationale: Retain the bounded authenticated protocol facade.

entries.name: Protocol codec
entries.rationale: Retain canonical CBOR encode/decode behavior.

entries.name: Protocol cryptography
entries.rationale: Retain HKDF/HMAC session and frame authentication.

entries.name: Protocol errors
entries.rationale: Retain explicit typed protocol failures.

entries.name: Authenticated protocol frames
entries.rationale: Retain bounded framing, sequence, direction, and authentication checks.

entries.name: Protocol handshake
entries.rationale: Retain mutual negotiation and readiness proof.

entries.name: Protocol frame limits
entries.rationale: Retain hard and negotiated frame bounds.

entries.name: Protocol message types
entries.rationale: Retain versioned message and capability discriminants.

entries.name: sendbox
entries.rationale: Preserve a single command entry point and stable help/exit contracts.

entries.name: sendbox run
entries.rationale: Preserve sandbox launch intent behind the Rust runtime stack.

entries.name: sendbox init
entries.rationale: Preserve configuration initialization with canonical v1 output.

entries.name: sendbox analyze
entries.rationale: Preserve project analysis and devcontainer generation.

entries.name: sendbox secrets
entries.rationale: Preserve the secret-management command group.

entries.name: sendbox secrets add
entries.rationale: Preserve interactive secret insertion without argv values.

entries.name: sendbox secrets remove
entries.rationale: Preserve named secret removal.

entries.name: sendbox secrets list
entries.rationale: Preserve name-only secret listing.

entries.name: sendbox policy
entries.rationale: Preserve the policy command group.

entries.name: sendbox policy show
entries.rationale: Preserve human-readable policy inspection.

entries.name: sendbox policy validate
entries.rationale: Preserve validation and add deterministic JSON automation output.

entries.name: sendbox mcp
entries.rationale: Preserve MCP inspection command grouping.

entries.name: sendbox mcp script
entries.rationale: Final architecture cannot depend on generated bpftrace/bootstrap scripts.

conformance.compatibility_note: Replaced by signed prebuilt guest/BPF artifact inspection.

entries.name: sendbox mcp parse
entries.rationale: Preserve native trace parsing with bounded input.

entries.name: sendbox mcp report
entries.rationale: Preserve summarized MCP reporting and redaction.

entries.name: sendbox boundary
entries.rationale: Retain inspection without runtime-generated executable artifacts.

conformance.compatibility_note: The group inspects signed plans/artifacts instead of emitting executable scripts.

entries.name: sendbox boundary script
entries.rationale: Generated bootstrap/proxy/seccomp executables are forbidden at cutover.

conformance.compatibility_note: Replaced by structured plan and artifact inspection.

entries.name: sendbox completions
entries.rationale: Preserve shell completion generation.

entries.name: sendbox completions install
entries.rationale: Preserve explicit completion installation.

entries.name: sendbox completions print
entries.rationale: Preserve deterministic completion output for bash, zsh, and fish.

entries.name: name
entries.rationale: Required human-readable sandbox identifier; no implicit default.

entries.name: project_path
entries.rationale: Required project root; canonical validation requires an absolute path.

entries.name: runtime
entries.rationale: Optional runtime section defaults to host auto-selection.

entries.name: runtime.provider
entries.rationale: Default auto; values auto, apple, kata, hyperlight.

entries.name: runtime.kata.executable
entries.rationale: Default nerdctl.

entries.name: runtime.kata.runtime_handler
entries.rationale: Default io.containerd.kata.v2.

entries.name: runtime.kata.namespace
entries.rationale: Default sendbox.

entries.name: runtime.kata.address
entries.rationale: Optional containerd address with no default.

entries.name: runtime.kata.snapshotter
entries.rationale: Optional snapshotter with no default.

entries.name: runtime.kata.configuration_path
entries.rationale: Optional absolute Kata configuration path.

entries.name: runtime.hyperlight.executable
entries.rationale: Default /usr/local/bin/hyperlight-unikraft.

entries.name: runtime.hyperlight.kernel_path
entries.rationale: Required absolute path for Hyperlight; empty default is invalid when selected.

entries.name: runtime.hyperlight.initrd_path
entries.rationale: Optional absolute initrd path.

entries.name: runtime.hyperlight.stack_mb
entries.rationale: Default 8 MiB and must be positive.

entries.name: resources
entries.rationale: Required resource section with v1 defaults from generated configs.

entries.name: resources.cpus
entries.rationale: Default 4 and must be positive.

entries.name: resources.memory_mb
entries.rationale: Default 4096 and must be positive.

entries.name: resources.disk_size_mb
entries.rationale: Default 10240 and must be positive.

entries.name: policy
entries.rationale: Required commands and network policy with defaulted boundaries.

entries.name: policy.commands.default_action
entries.rationale: Required allow or deny action; generated default deny.

entries.name: policy.commands.allowlist
entries.rationale: Required ordered list of command patterns.

entries.name: policy.commands.denylist
entries.rationale: Required ordered list; deny matches take priority.

entries.name: policy.commands.log_blocked
entries.rationale: Required boolean; generated default true.

entries.name: policy.network.default_action
entries.rationale: Required allow or deny action; generated default deny.

entries.name: policy.network.allowed_domains
entries.rationale: Required ordered domain patterns.

entries.name: policy.network.blocked_domains
entries.rationale: Required ordered domain patterns; blocked wins.

entries.name: policy.network.allow_dns
entries.rationale: Required boolean; generated default true.

entries.name: policy.network.max_connections
entries.rationale: Optional positive connection bound.

entries.name: policy.boundaries
entries.rationale: Default enabled, but semantics move to signed guest services.

conformance.compatibility_note: Configuration remains readable while unsupported legacy generated boundaries receive migration errors.

entries.name: policy.boundaries.enabled
entries.rationale: Default true; false explicitly disables mandatory boundary features.

entries.name: policy.boundaries.tool_calls.transport
entries.rationale: Default and only current value stdio.

entries.name: policy.boundaries.tool_calls.default_action
entries.rationale: Default deny.

entries.name: policy.boundaries.tool_calls.allowlist
entries.rationale: Default empty tool patterns.

entries.name: policy.boundaries.tool_calls.denylist
entries.rationale: Default empty tool patterns; deny wins.

entries.name: policy.boundaries.tool_calls.max_frame_bytes
entries.rationale: Default 1048576 and must be positive.

entries.name: policy.boundaries.tool_calls.server_command_patterns
entries.rationale: Default known MCP server markers.

entries.name: policy.boundaries.tool_calls.allowed_server_commands
entries.rationale: Default empty exact argv allowlist.

entries.name: policy.boundaries.syscalls.additional_denylist
entries.rationale: Default empty additions; required lifecycle syscalls cannot be denied.

entries.name: policy.boundaries.syscalls.log_blocked
entries.rationale: Default true.

entries.name: policy.boundaries.log_path
entries.rationale: Default /var/log/sendbox/boundary.log and must be absolute.

entries.name: secrets
entries.rationale: Required list of nonempty secret names; values never appear in config.

entries.name: devcontainer.config_path
entries.rationale: Optional existing devcontainer path.

entries.name: devcontainer.auto_generate
entries.rationale: Generated config default true.

entries.name: devcontainer.extensions
entries.rationale: Generated config default empty list.

entries.name: github.forward_auth
entries.rationale: Required boolean; generated default true.

entries.name: github.forward_copilot_auth
entries.rationale: Required boolean; generated default true.

entries.name: github.allow_private_repository_access
entries.rationale: Default false.

entries.name: github.branch_protection.enabled
entries.rationale: Default true and requires boundaries.

entries.name: github.branch_protection.username
entries.rationale: Optional username; default auto-detection.

entries.name: github.branch_protection.protected_branches
entries.rationale: Default main and master.

entries.name: github.branch_protection.allowed_branch_patterns
entries.rationale: Defaults username, copilot, and feature prefixes.

entries.name: github.ssh_key_path
entries.rationale: Optional SSH forwarding needs a separate credential threat model.

conformance.compatibility_note: Config remains readable; unsupported use receives an explicit capability error.

entries.name: observability.mcp_inspection.enabled
entries.rationale: Default false.

entries.name: observability.mcp_inspection.transports
entries.rationale: Defaults stdio and http, but support must reflect proven capture capability.

conformance.compatibility_note: HTTP may remain observation-only and cannot be presented as enforcement.

entries.name: observability.mcp_inspection.capture_payloads
entries.rationale: Default true; false requires metadata-only redaction.

entries.name: observability.mcp_inspection.max_payload_bytes
entries.rationale: Default 16384 and must be positive.

entries.name: observability.mcp_inspection.log_path
entries.rationale: Default /var/log/sendbox/mcp-trace.log and must be absolute.

entries.name: observability.mcp_inspection.server_command_patterns
entries.rationale: Default known MCP server process markers.

entries.name: availability
entries.rationale: Runtime availability must be explicit and side-effect free.

entries.name: pull image
entries.rationale: Preserve explicit image acquisition with streamed diagnostics.

entries.name: create
entries.rationale: Creation must not launch agent code before controls are ready.

conformance.compatibility_note: Creation and agent launch become separate state transitions.

entries.name: start
entries.rationale: Start must complete mandatory guest readiness before agent launch.

conformance.compatibility_note: Start returns only after required readiness proof.

entries.name: stop
entries.rationale: Preserve graceful stop with bounded escalation.

entries.name: remove
entries.rationale: Preserve idempotent resource removal.

entries.name: status
entries.rationale: Preserve typed lifecycle status reporting.

entries.name: exec
entries.rationale: Route semantic execution through the trusted broker.

conformance.compatibility_note: Observable argv/exit/stream behavior remains while authorization moves before exec.

entries.name: logs
entries.rationale: Preserve bounded stdout/stderr retrieval.

entries.name: streamed I/O
entries.rationale: Preserve independent stdout/stderr streaming and backpressure.

entries.name: signals and cancellation
entries.rationale: Preserve SIGINT/cancellation propagation with deterministic cleanup.

entries.name: idempotent cleanup
entries.rationale: Cleanup must survive partial startup, cancellation, and process death.

conformance.compatibility_note: Cleanup becomes an explicit transaction rather than best-effort orchestration.

entries.name: sandbox YAML
entries.rationale: Retain v1 reader and canonical migration report.

entries.name: protocol CBOR
entries.rationale: Retain version 1 canonical bounded wire format.

entries.name: audit export JSON
entries.rationale: Current unversioned Swift export requires a versioned reader and migration policy.

conformance.compatibility_note: Old records remain verifiable through a dedicated legacy reader.

entries.name: snapshot manifest
entries.rationale: Current unversioned Codable form requires safe versioned traversal metadata.

conformance.compatibility_note: Legacy manifests require explicit fixture capture and migration.

entries.name: provenance signature
entries.rationale: Version signer, trust, digest, and artifact metadata.

conformance.compatibility_note: Existing signatures require a documented verification lifetime.

entries.name: supervisor grant
entries.rationale: Version approval scope, expiry, pattern, and audit linkage.

conformance.compatibility_note: Existing Codable grants are observations until fixtures are captured.

entries.name: setup.sh
entries.rationale: Replace shell setup with native Rust preflight and wizard.

conformance.compatibility_note: Native setup preserves user-visible checks without shell dependency.

entries.name: make install
entries.rationale: Preserve source installation behavior during migration.

entries.name: make install-completions
entries.rationale: Preserve explicit completion installation.

entries.name: bash completion
entries.rationale: Generate from the Rust clap command tree.

entries.name: zsh completion
entries.rationale: Generate from the Rust clap command tree.

entries.name: fish completion
entries.rationale: Generate from the Rust clap command tree.

entries.name: macOS tarball
entries.rationale: Continue publishing a portable host binary archive.

entries.name: macOS pkg
entries.rationale: Continue publishing an installer package with explicit signing status.

entries.name: macOS dmg
entries.rationale: Continue publishing a disk image when release signing is qualified.

entries.name: copilot-bridge
entries.rationale: Local project analysis becomes native Rust and model refinement is optional.

conformance.compatibility_note: No mandatory Node runtime remains at cutover.

entries.name: File isolation
entries.rationale: Retain explicit mount-only visibility contract.

entries.name: Command filtering
entries.rationale: Limit claim to broker-compatible semantic execution plus kernel containment.

conformance.compatibility_note: Universal binary filtering claims are removed.

entries.name: Network firewall
entries.rationale: Claim only protocols proven pre-connect through egress enforcement.

conformance.compatibility_note: Unsupported UDP/QUIC/direct-IP combinations fail validation.

entries.name: Runtime providers
entries.rationale: Retain Apple, Kata, and Hyperlight selection with capability disclosure.

entries.name: Hyperlight execution
entries.rationale: Retain qualified one-shot commands and MCP servers.

entries.name: Credential injection
entries.rationale: Preserve non-persistence while adding encrypted transfer and strict scope.

conformance.compatibility_note: Storage identifiers are preserved where safe; transport is replaced.

entries.name: Undo and rollback
entries.rationale: Retain operations with safe traversal and versioned formats.

conformance.compatibility_note: Legacy snapshot compatibility requires captured fixtures.

entries.name: Audit trail
entries.rationale: Retain integrity verification with versioned append-only events.

conformance.compatibility_note: Claims are limited to events actually wired into the lifecycle.

entries.name: MCP inspection
entries.rationale: Separate native parsing and observability from authorization.

conformance.compatibility_note: HTTP/TLS capture cannot be described as enforcement.

entries.name: Boundary enforcement
entries.rationale: Use signed guest services and fail-closed pre-action authorization.

conformance.compatibility_note: Generated Python/bpftrace/C bootstrap is removed.

entries.name: Supply chain provenance
entries.rationale: Extend from config signing to signed reproducible release artifacts.

conformance.compatibility_note: Trust metadata and verification results become versioned.

entries.name: Runtime supervisor
entries.rationale: Retain grants through an explicit auditable state machine.

conformance.compatibility_note: Only lifecycle-wired approvals may be claimed.

entries.name: VM hardening
entries.rationale: Use service-specific typed profiles with live architecture tests.

conformance.compatibility_note: Benchmark-scenario coverage is not claimed without live enforcement tests.

entries.name: Devcontainer generation
entries.rationale: Retain deterministic generation from native project analysis.

entries.name: Hardware-isolated sandboxing
entries.rationale: Qualify the claim per runtime and host capability.

conformance.compatibility_note: Unsupported or software-only modes must be disclosed explicitly.

entries.name: Deny-by-default posture
entries.rationale: Retain explicit default-deny semantics across policy domains.
